description: Canonical parameter dictionary for switching DC-DC regulators, synthesized from a 20-datasheet corpus (TI, Analog Devices, MPS, Richtek, Aerosemi) spanning FOUR sub-shapes that share one measurement envelope: (1) monolithic converters with integrated FETs (buck LM2596/TPS54560/LMR33630/TPS563201/MP2315/LMR51430/TPS62840; boost MT3608/LM2577/TPS61023/TPS61088; buck-boost RT6150/TPS63020/LTC3113) which populate switch on-resistance, current limits, and switching keys; (2) controllers driving external FETs (LM5116/LM25116/TPS40057/LTC3891) which populate gate-driver (peak source/sink current OR pull-up/pull-down resistance), current-sense-threshold, internal-regulator, and emulated-ramp keys instead; (3) power modules with integrated inductor (LMZ31710) which populate efficiency, ripple, transient-response, and setpoint-tolerance keys; and (4) isolated primary-side-sensing flyback controllers (LT3748) which have no VOUT pin and no feedback divider - output voltage is inferred from the primary flyback pulse via an RFB/RREF resistor ratio times the transformer turns ratio - and populate the flyback-feedback keys (tc_current, flyback_comparator_trip_current, transformer_turns_ratio, leakage_inductance) plus boundary/critical-conduction-mode timers (max_on_time, max_off_time, max_discontinuous_off_time). A part's sub_type selects which optional keys apply; the core switching/reference/protection keys are common. The error amplifier appears in two idioms: transconductance (unit S, gm-controllers) OR DC gain (dB or V/V) plus unity-gain bandwidth (voltage-mode) - a part populates one. Condition axes for this family: V_IN, V_OUT, V_FB, V_SW, V_CC, V_CCX, V_SS, V_EN, V_C (compensation/control voltage), V_SENSE, V_RREF, F_SW, R_T, R_ILIM, R_TC, C_SS, C_RAMP, DUTY, MODE (PFM/PWM/pass-through/boundary, note-only), SWITCH (buck-boost H-bridge switch A/B/C/D, note-only), DRIVER (gate-driver HO/LO or TG/BG, note-only), DIRECTION (rising/falling/source/sink, note-only), N_PS (transformer turns ratio, note-only), PACKAGE, T_J, T_A. Pin functions include SW/SW1/SW2 (switch node[s]), BOOT/BST/HB (bootstrap), TG/BG/HO/LO/GATE (gate drives), COMP/ITH (compensation), SS, RT/FREQ (timing/sync), VCC/VCCX/EXTVCC (internal + external bias), CS/CSG/SENSE (current sense), SENSE_P/SENSE_N (differential sense pair), PGND/AGND/SGND (split grounds), PVIN (power vs bias input), ILIM, MODE/SYNC, PG, and the flyback-specific TC/RREF/RFB (temperature-comp, reference, and primary-flyback-feedback resistors). Base-SI throughout; the inductance unit H is used for a recommended or integrated inductor and for transformer primary/leakage inductance. Line/load regulation follows the LDO convention: absolute V by default, with %, V/V, or ppm/V (line) and % or ohm (load) permitted. A dimensionless ratio (turns ratio, current-limit range ratio) uses V/V. Rail-relative specs (a gate-drive output stated as a drop from an internal rail, a power-good or OVP window as a percent of the setpoint) are stored as the magnitude with the printed form in conditionsVerbatim. Efficiency, line/load regulation, and dropout are frequently graph-only on cheaper parts; keep them optional. Aliases normalize the printed datasheet symbols.

parameters.name: Input voltage range
parameters.hint: The operating input-voltage range (recommended), and/or the absolute-maximum input voltage, distinguished by limitClass. A module or split-rail controller may separate the power input (PVIN) from the bias input (VIN); note which in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Output voltage range
parameters.hint: The settable output-voltage range for an adjustable part (set by an external FB divider). A fixed-output variant pins one value; use component.orderingVariants.outputVoltageV for that.

parameters.name: Quiescent current
parameters.hint: Non-switching operating supply current, a headline spec for low-IQ parts (nanoamps on nano-power buck). Strongly mode-dependent (PFM vs forced-PWM vs not-switching); give a MODE note and one measurement per mode. Distinguish current into VIN vs VOUT when both are given.

parameters.name: Shutdown current
parameters.hint: Supply current with the part disabled (EN low).

parameters.name: Reference / feedback voltage
parameters.hint: The internal reference the feedback divider regulates to. Can differ slightly between PWM and PFM modes (give a MODE note). For a resistor-programmed (R2D) part the reference is not directly tabulated; capture the set output under output_voltage_range instead.

parameters.name: Feedback input current
parameters.hint: Bias/leakage current into the feedback pin, which sets the divider error.

parameters.name: Temperature-compensation current
parameters.hint: Isolated flyback (primary-side sensing): current sourced from the TC pin into the RREF node to cancel the output rectifier's forward-voltage temperature drift (ITC = VTC / RTC). Set by an external RTC resistor (array over R_TC).

parameters.name: Flyback sampling-comparator trip current
parameters.hint: Isolated flyback (primary-side sensing): the current into the RFB (primary-flyback-feedback) pin at which the sampling comparator trips, which - with RREF and the transformer turns ratio - sets the regulated output voltage. These parts have no feedback divider and no VOUT pin.

parameters.name: High-side switch on-resistance
parameters.hint: On-resistance of the integrated high-side FET (monolithic converters only; a controller drives external FETs and omits this). Package- and gate-drive-dependent, so note VBST-VSW or VCC. For a boost the high-side FET is the rectifier - alias both namings.

parameters.name: Low-side switch on-resistance
parameters.hint: On-resistance of the integrated low-side (synchronous rectifier) FET. For a boost the low-side FET is the main switch.

parameters.name: Switch saturation voltage
parameters.hint: Non-synchronous converters with a bipolar (BJT) power switch: the switch saturation voltage at a stated current, in place of an RDS(on).

parameters.name: Switch leakage current
parameters.hint: Off-state leakage of the power switch / switch node at a stated switch-node voltage.

parameters.name: High-side (peak) current limit
parameters.hint: Peak (high-side or main-switch) current limit. May be mode-dependent (PFM vs FPWM) or externally programmed by an ILIM resistor; note the condition.

parameters.name: Low-side (valley) current limit
parameters.hint: Valley (low-side) current limit for a synchronous converter.

parameters.name: Negative current limit
parameters.hint: Reverse (sink) current limit in forced-PWM mode.

parameters.name: Current-sense threshold voltage
parameters.hint: Controllers only: the cycle-by-cycle current-limit threshold as a sense voltage across the external sense element (the limit current is set externally by Rsense). Not a current.

parameters.name: Overcurrent / short-circuit threshold
parameters.hint: A hard overcurrent/short-circuit fault threshold distinct from the cycle-by-cycle limit: a sense voltage across the external sense element (V) on a controller, or an absolute current (A) on a monolithic part. May be a fixed multiple of the current-limit threshold; note the V_C or ratio.

parameters.name: Current-sense input bias current
parameters.hint: Bias/leakage current into a current-sense input (SENSE, CS/CSG). Loads the external sense element and shifts the effective limit; often specified at a stated sense-pin voltage (array over V_SENSE).

parameters.name: Current-limit threshold range ratio
parameters.hint: Dimensionless ratio (V/V) of the maximum to minimum current-sense threshold as the control/compensation voltage is swept, on a part whose current limit is set by VC. A guaranteed table row on some current-mode controllers, not a derived quantity.

parameters.name: Minimum peak inductor current
parameters.hint: The minimum controllable peak inductor (switch) current per cycle, which with the minimum on-time bounds the smallest deliverable pulse and sets light-load behavior.

parameters.name: Zero-cross / zero-current detector threshold
parameters.hint: The threshold at which the synchronous rectifier turns off in discontinuous/diode-emulation mode, as an inductor current (A) or an equivalent switch-node sense voltage (V). Usually slightly negative.

parameters.name: Switching frequency
parameters.hint: The switching (oscillator) frequency. Programmable parts give it vs the RT resistor (array over R_T) or as frequency-option variants; a fixed part gives one value. Give the RT value or the option in a condition.

parameters.name: Synchronization frequency range
parameters.hint: The range over which an external clock on the SYNC/RT pin can set the switching frequency.

parameters.name: Minimum on-time
parameters.hint: Minimum controllable high-side on-time, which sets the maximum step-down ratio at a given frequency.

parameters.name: Minimum off-time
parameters.hint: Minimum off-time, which bounds the maximum duty cycle in dropout.

parameters.name: Maximum on-time
parameters.hint: Maximum high-side (or main-switch) on-time, often an internal watchdog/restart timer on boundary-mode and isolated controllers that lack a fixed clock.

parameters.name: Maximum off-time
parameters.hint: Maximum off-time / restart timer that forces a switching cycle after an idle interval, on boundary-mode and isolated controllers. On a monolithic buck a 'forced off-time' that sets the maximum duty cycle also belongs here; note it.

parameters.name: Maximum discontinuous off-time
parameters.hint: Boundary/critical-conduction-mode controllers: the maximum off-time in discontinuous operation before a new cycle is initiated (distinct from the absolute restart timer).

parameters.name: Emulated-ramp generator current
parameters.hint: Emulated peak-current-mode controllers: the current that charges the external RAMP capacitor to synthesize the inductor-current ramp. Often specified at two (V_IN, V_OUT) operating points that bracket the slope; one measurement per point.

parameters.name: Maximum duty cycle
parameters.hint: Maximum duty cycle. 100% signals a non-synchronous / low-dropout-capable part. May be frequency-dependent.

parameters.name: Dead time
parameters.hint: Switch-node dead time between high-side and low-side conduction. Often two directional values (LO-fall-to-HO-rise and HO-fall-to-LO-rise); use a DIRECTION note.

parameters.name: Error-amplifier transconductance
parameters.hint: Transconductance-amplifier controllers/converters: the error-amp gm in siemens (a printed uA/V or umho is siemens). A voltage-mode part instead specifies error_amp_dc_gain and error_amp_bandwidth; a part populates one idiom.

parameters.name: Error-amplifier DC gain
parameters.hint: Voltage-mode error amplifiers: open-loop DC gain, paired with error_amp_bandwidth. Store in dB, or as a dimensionless V/V ratio when the datasheet prints it that way (do not convert and lose the printed form; keep it in conditionsVerbatim).

parameters.name: Error-amplifier unity-gain bandwidth
parameters.hint: Unity-gain bandwidth of a voltage-mode error amplifier.

parameters.name: Error-amplifier output current
parameters.hint: COMP-pin source/sink drive current; use one measurement per direction with a DIRECTION note when given separately.

parameters.name: Gate-driver peak source current
parameters.hint: Controllers only: peak source current of a gate-driver output (HO/LO or HDRV/LDRV); note which driver.

parameters.name: Gate-driver peak sink current
parameters.hint: Controllers only: peak sink current of a gate-driver output; note which driver.

parameters.name: Gate-driver output voltage (drop)
parameters.hint: Controllers only: gate-driver output low voltage, or the high-side/low-side output drop below its rail (VOH given as VBP10-VLDRV etc.). Store the drop magnitude and keep the relative-to-rail form in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Gate-driver rise / fall time
parameters.hint: Controllers only: gate-driver output rise/fall time at a stated gate load capacitance (a C_L condition). One measurement per edge/driver.

parameters.name: Gate-driver pull-up resistance
parameters.hint: Controllers only: gate-driver output pull-up (source) on-resistance, the resistance idiom for driver strength used in place of a peak source current. One measurement per driver (a DRIVER note: TG/HO or BG/LO).

parameters.name: Gate-driver pull-down resistance
parameters.hint: Controllers only: gate-driver output pull-down (sink) on-resistance, used in place of a peak sink current. One measurement per driver (a DRIVER note: TG/HO or BG/LO).

parameters.name: Bootstrap undervoltage threshold
parameters.hint: The bootstrap (high-side gate-drive supply) undervoltage-lockout threshold, referenced to the switch node.

parameters.name: Internal regulator (VCC) voltage
parameters.hint: The internal bias/gate-drive regulator (VCC, or BP5/BP10 on some parts) output voltage. Drops out at low VIN, so note VIN.

parameters.name: Internal regulator (VCC) UVLO
parameters.hint: Undervoltage-lockout threshold on the internal VCC regulator (distinct from the line UVLO).

parameters.name: External bias switchover threshold
parameters.hint: Controllers with a VCCX external-bias input: the threshold at which the part switches from the internal VCC regulator to the external bias rail (for efficiency).

parameters.name: Internal regulator (VCC) dropout voltage
parameters.hint: The VIN-to-VCC dropout of the internal bias/gate-drive regulator at a stated VCC load current; sets the minimum VIN at which the drivers stay in regulation.

parameters.name: External bias switchover hysteresis
parameters.hint: Hysteresis on the VCCX/EXTVCC external-bias switchover threshold.

parameters.name: Internal regulator (VCC) current limit
parameters.hint: The current-limit (sourcing capability) of the internal VCC bias regulator, which bounds the total gate-drive charge the part can supply.

parameters.name: Soft-start time
parameters.hint: Output ramp time at start-up. Fixed internally on some parts, or set by an external soft-start cap (array over C_SS).

parameters.name: Soft-start charging current
parameters.hint: The current that charges the external soft-start capacitor to set the ramp.

parameters.name: Soft-start switching-start threshold
parameters.hint: The soft-start pin voltage at which switching begins (an initial offset the SS capacitor must charge through before the output ramps).

parameters.name: Soft-start reset (discharge) current
parameters.hint: The current that discharges the external soft-start capacitor on a fault/restart, which sets the re-start interval.

parameters.name: Enable threshold voltage
parameters.hint: Enable-pin threshold(s). A precision-enable part gives separate rising and falling values (use DIRECTION); an active-low SHDN part inverts the sense (note it).

parameters.name: Enable hysteresis
parameters.hint: Hysteresis on the enable threshold (voltage or via a hysteresis current, noted separately).

parameters.name: Enable input current
parameters.hint: Current into the enable pin, often specified at two pin voltages (above/below threshold); one measurement per V_EN point.

parameters.name: UVLO / enable hysteresis current
parameters.hint: Parts that program UVLO with a resistor divider set hysteresis with a switched pull-down/pull-up current (rather than a voltage) at the threshold; capture it here (enable_hysteresis stays the voltage idiom).

parameters.name: Input UVLO threshold
parameters.hint: Line (input) undervoltage-lockout threshold; give the rising and falling values via DIRECTION. On a controller this may be an externally programmed threshold via a UVLO pin resistor.

parameters.name: Input UVLO hysteresis
parameters.hint: Hysteresis on the input UVLO threshold.

parameters.name: Output overvoltage-protection threshold
parameters.hint: Output overvoltage-protection threshold (common on boost/buck-boost). May be an absolute voltage (V) or a percent of the reference/setpoint (%); store the percent when the datasheet states it that way, mirroring power_good_threshold.

parameters.name: Thermal shutdown threshold
parameters.hint: Junction temperature at which the part shuts down (rising).

parameters.name: Thermal shutdown hysteresis
parameters.hint: Temperature drop below the shutdown threshold before the part recovers.

parameters.name: Thermal shutdown recovery temperature
parameters.hint: The (falling) junction temperature at which the part restarts after a thermal shutdown, when the datasheet tabulates the recovery point directly instead of a hysteresis. Prefer capturing this tabulated value over subtracting to synthesize a hysteresis.

parameters.name: Short-circuit (hiccup) trip threshold
parameters.hint: The feedback-voltage threshold below which a persistent-overload hiccup mode is entered (distinct from the cycle-by-cycle current limit).

parameters.name: Hiccup restart time
parameters.hint: Off/wait time between hiccup restart attempts during a persistent short-circuit fault.

parameters.name: Power-good threshold
parameters.hint: Power-good comparator threshold(s), usually as a percent of the reference/setpoint (upper OV and lower UV, each rising and falling). Store the percent; a part that gives an absolute voltage may use V.

parameters.name: Power-good deglitch delay
parameters.hint: Deglitch/filter delay on the power-good signal.

parameters.name: Power-good output-low voltage
parameters.hint: Open-drain power-good output-low voltage at a stated sink current.

parameters.name: Power-good leakage current
parameters.hint: Open-drain power-good off-state leakage current.

parameters.name: Power-good output on-resistance
parameters.hint: Open-drain power-good output on-resistance (an alternative to a stated output-low voltage). May differ enabled vs disabled (array over V_EN).

parameters.name: Efficiency
parameters.hint: Conversion efficiency. Usually a graph (out of scope); capture only tabulated points, one measurement per (V_IN, V_OUT, F_SW, I_OUT) operating point. Common on modules and some converters, absent on many.

parameters.name: Dropout voltage
parameters.hint: The minimum input-to-output differential at which the converter maintains regulation (a low-dropout / 100%-duty-capable buck), at a stated load current and output voltage. Distinct from max_duty_cycle.

parameters.name: Line regulation
parameters.hint: Output (or reference) change over the input-voltage range, as absolute volts, a percent of Vout, a normalized V/V, or ppm/V for a reference-grade line regulation. Often graph-only; capture only tabulated values.

parameters.name: Load regulation
parameters.hint: Output change over the load range, as absolute volts, a percent of Vout, or an effective ohm (dVout/dIout). Often graph-only.

parameters.name: Output voltage accuracy
parameters.hint: Setpoint accuracy / total output-voltage variation as a percent (module-level parts combine setpoint, line, load, and temperature).

parameters.name: Output voltage ripple
parameters.hint: Peak-to-peak output ripple (set statistic to peak_to_peak) over a stated measurement bandwidth. Module-level.

parameters.name: Load-transient recovery time
parameters.hint: Recovery time after a load step (use the stimulus field for the step). Module-level.

parameters.name: Load-transient voltage deviation
parameters.hint: Peak output over/undershoot for a stated load step. Module-level.

parameters.name: Recommended / integrated inductance
parameters.hint: The recommended external inductance range (in a guaranteed Recommended-Operating table), or the integrated inductance of a module. Base-SI henries: 2.2 uH is 2.2e-06.

parameters.name: Transformer turns ratio
parameters.hint: Isolated (flyback/forward) topologies: the recommended transformer primary-to-secondary turns ratio, a dimensionless value (V/V). Enters the output-voltage equation and every voltage-stress calculation; belongs to the external transformer, so capture the recommended value(s).

parameters.name: Transformer leakage inductance
parameters.hint: Isolated topologies: the recommended/maximum transformer primary leakage inductance, which sets the snubber requirement and switch voltage stress. Base-SI henries.

parameters.name: Recommended input capacitance
parameters.hint: The recommended/required input capacitance.

parameters.name: Recommended output capacitance
parameters.hint: The recommended output capacitance range.

parameters.name: Bias/decoupling capacitance
parameters.hint: A required decoupling/bootstrap capacitance (VCC bypass, bootstrap cap).

parameters.name: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Per-package (and board) junction-to-ambient thermal resistance.

parameters.name: Junction-to-case thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-case thermal resistance (top).

parameters.name: Junction-to-board thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-board thermal resistance.

parameters.name: Power dissipation
parameters.hint: Maximum internal power dissipation (module-level, or an absolute-maximum row).

parameters.name: Operating temperature range
parameters.hint: Specified operating (junction or free-air) temperature range.

parameters.name: Junction temperature (absolute maximum)
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum junction temperature.

parameters.name: Storage temperature range
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum storage temperature range.

parameters.name: ESD, human body model
parameters.hint: ESD withstand, human body model. Store as absolute_max.

parameters.name: ESD, charged device model
parameters.hint: ESD withstand, charged device model. Store as absolute_max.
